title: Ricinocarpus liebmannii
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus liebmannii is classified within the family Haloragaceae and order Haloragales. It is a genus of aquatic plants native to South America. The exact classification of Ricinocarpus liebmannii can be broken down further into its rank. Ricinocarpus liebmannii is classified as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Haloragales, family Haloragaceae, genus Ricinocarpus, species R. liebmannii.
uses: Ricinocarpus liebmannii has been traditionally used in Mexican medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions.
